title: Exodus: Golden Calf and Renewal
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of exodus: golden calf and renewal and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study exodus: golden calf and renewal and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What did Aaron make for Israel while Moses was receiving the law on the mountain? Answer: a golden calf for the people to worship.
acceptable_variants: an idol of gold in the shape of a calf
a golden image of a calf that they worshipped
aaron cast a golden calf for israel to worship

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The idol Aaron made in Exodus 32 while Moses was on Sinai; a direct violation of the first two commandments and a precursor to Jeroboam's golden calves in 1 Kings 12
Moses' repeated prayers on behalf of sinful Israel; in Exodus 32-34, he pleads for God not to destroy the people and models the prophetic role of mediator
